task.title: Preserve event-log error sources
task.objective: Complete issue https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/issues/350 by letting Rust error-reporting layers traverse the SQLite and JSON causes wrapped by EventLogError without changing its public variants or display text.
acceptance_criteria: Storage and encoding variants expose their wrapped errors through std::error::Error::source.
Optimistic-concurrency and range failures remain source-free.
Existing public variants and display text remain unchanged.
Tests and event-log documentation describe the error-chain behavior.
The pinned local and required pull-request quality gates pass.

attempts.summary: Added a focused source-chain test before changing EventLogError.
attempts.diagnostic: The RED test panicked when unwrapping storage.source() because the empty Error implementation returned None for every variant.
attempts.patch: Constructed all four EventLogError variants and asserted concrete source types for wrapped failures and no source for domain failures.

attempts.summary: Implemented variant-specific source delegation and documented the compatibility contract.
attempts.patch: Returned the borrowed rusqlite or serde_json error for Storage and Encode while returning None for WrongExpectedVersion and VersionOutOfRange.

attempts.summary: Kept the fix bounded after independent diff review and automated pull-request review.
attempts.diagnostic: No blocking finding remained; CodeRabbit generated no actionable comments.
attempts.patch: Preserved variants, formatting, dependencies, and replay behavior; only source chaining, its test, and documentation changed.

outcome.summary: EventLogError now composes with conventional Rust error chains while retaining source-free domain failures and its existing compatibility surface.

verification.command: GitHub Rust quality gate for pull request 351

lessons: An Error implementation is not fully composable when wrapper variants omit source delegation.
Test both sides of an error-chain contract: wrapped infrastructure failures should expose causes, while domain failures without underlying errors should return None.
Borrowing existing inner errors through source improves diagnostics without changing display text, variants, dependencies, or persisted behavior.
